mnemonics: Master of Amazing Processes, Reduce the Output Now
Machines Align Parallelly, Reduce in Harmony
keyPoints: MapReduce: A programming model used for processing large data sets
Mapper: Splits data into smaller chunks, assigns key-value pairs to each chunk
Reducer: Takes the output from Mapper, reduces it to a single value or summary
Combiner: Accelerates the processing of large data sets by combining smaller chunks
Partitioner: Divides data into smaller chunks for Map and Reduce operations
Searching: A key component of MapReduce, used for finding data within large datasets
Sorting: Used in MapReduce to organize data in a specific order
Compression: Used to reduce the size of data, making it easier to store and transfer
